title: Buggery Act makes male homosexuality a capital crime in England
description: Under Henry VIII the Buggery Act of 1533 made sexual acts between men punishable by death in England. It was the first secular law in the country to criminalise such acts and shaped later legislation across Britain and its colonies.

sources.label: High Speed Training — History of LGBTQ+ Timeline

title: Last executions for homosexuality in Britain
description: The final executions carried out for homosexual acts in Britain took place in 1835. Capital punishment for such offences was formally removed shortly afterwards.

sources.label: High Speed Training — History of LGBTQ+ Timeline

title: Roberta Cowell has her sex legally changed to female
description: In 1951 Roberta Cowell, a British trans woman, underwent gender reassignment surgery and had her legal sex changed to female. She is regarded as one of the earliest British trans women to do so.

sources.label: High Speed Training — History of LGBTQ+ Timeline

title: North Western Homosexual Law Reform Committee is established
description: The North Western Homosexual Law Reform Committee formed in 1964 to campaign for legal and social equality for lesbian, gay and bisexual people. It later became the Campaign for Homosexual Equality.

sources.label: High Speed Training — History of LGBTQ+ Timeline

title: The Beaumont Society forms as a trans support group
description: Founded in 1966, the Beaumont Society provided information and self-help support for transgender people in Britain and remains one of the country's longest-running trans organisations.

sources.label: High Speed Training — History of LGBTQ+ Timeline

title: Sexual Offences Act partially decriminalises sex between men
description: The Sexual Offences Act 1967 decriminalised private sexual acts between two men aged over 21 in England and Wales. Discrimination and unequal treatment persisted after the change.

sources.label: High Speed Training — History of LGBTQ+ Timeline

title: Stonewall riots break out in New York City
description: The Stonewall riots began on 28 June 1969 after a police raid on the Stonewall Inn in New York City. The uprising galvanised the modern gay rights movement and led to the formation of the Gay Liberation Front.

sources.label: High Speed Training — History of LGBTQ+ Timeline

title: London Gay Liberation Front is established
description: The London Gay Liberation Front formed in 1970, modelled on the United States group of the same name, and became a driving force in early British gay activism.

sources.label: High Speed Training — History of LGBTQ+ Timeline

title: First London Pride march is held
description: In 1972 the Gay Liberation Front organised the first London Pride march to mark the anniversary of Stonewall, drawing around 2,000 participants.

sources.label: High Speed Training — History of LGBTQ+ Timeline

title: Gilbert Baker designs the rainbow Pride flag
description: The artist Gilbert Baker created the rainbow Pride flag in 1978, giving the movement an enduring symbol. His original design featured eight colours.

sources.label: High Speed Training — History of LGBTQ+ Timeline

title: Harry Benjamin International Gender Dysphoria Association is founded
description: The Harry Benjamin International Gender Dysphoria Association was set up in 1979 to advance transgender health care. It later became the World Professional Association for Transgender Health.

sources.label: High Speed Training — History of LGBTQ+ Timeline

title: Lifetime blood donation ban imposed on men who have sex with men
description: In 1985 the United Kingdom introduced a lifetime ban on blood donation by men who have sex with men, a response to the HIV/AIDS crisis that remained in force for decades.

sources.label: High Speed Training — History of LGBTQ+ Timeline

title: Section 28 restricts discussion of homosexuality
description: Section 28 of the Local Government Act came into force in 1988, barring local authorities in Britain from promoting homosexuality or presenting it as acceptable in schools. It had a chilling effect on education and support services.

sources.label: High Speed Training — History of LGBTQ+ Timeline

title: Stonewall UK is founded in response to Section 28
description: The campaigning group Stonewall was set up in 1988 by Ian McKellen, Lisa Power and Michael Cashman, among others, to oppose Section 28 and lobby for LGBTQ+ equality.

sources.label: High Speed Training — History of LGBTQ+ Timeline

title: OutRage! campaign group is formed
description: The direct-action group OutRage! was created in 1990 after a candlelit vigil for five gay men murdered in London. It campaigned against police mistreatment of LGBTQ+ people.

sources.label: High Speed Training — History of LGBTQ+ Timeline

title: World Health Organisation declassifies homosexuality as a mental illness
description: The World Health Organisation removed homosexuality from its classification of mental illnesses, a change reflected in the ICD-10. The reform ended the international medical framing of same-sex attraction as a disorder.

sources.label: High Speed Training — History of LGBTQ+ Timeline

title: Age of consent for sex between men lowered to 18
description: In 1994 Britain lowered the age of consent for sexual acts between men from 21 to 18, narrowing but not ending the gap with the age of consent for heterosexual couples.

sources.label: High Speed Training — History of LGBTQ+ Timeline

title: Ban on LGB people serving in the military is lifted
description: In 2000 the United Kingdom government ended the prohibition on lesbian, gay and bisexual people serving in the armed forces.

sources.label: High Speed Training — History of LGBTQ+ Timeline

title: Same-sex couples gain equal adoption rights
description: In 2002 same-sex couples in the United Kingdom won the right to adopt children on the same basis as heterosexual couples.

sources.label: High Speed Training — History of LGBTQ+ Timeline

title: Section 28 is repealed
description: Section 28 was repealed in England, Wales and Northern Ireland in 2003, ending the ban on local authorities presenting homosexuality positively. Scotland had already repealed the provision in 2000.

sources.label: High Speed Training — History of LGBTQ+ Timeline

title: Gender Recognition Act passes in Britain
description: The Gender Recognition Act 2004 allowed transgender people in Britain to gain legal recognition in their chosen gender and to obtain a new birth certificate.

sources.label: High Speed Training — History of LGBTQ+ Timeline

title: Lifetime blood donation ban on MSM is lifted
description: In 2011 the United Kingdom replaced the lifetime blood donation ban on men who have sex with men with a one-year deferral period. Northern Ireland retained the lifetime ban until 2016.

sources.label: High Speed Training — History of LGBTQ+ Timeline

title: Stonewall launches the "Gay. Let's get over it" schools campaign
description: In 2013 Stonewall UK ran its "Gay. Let's get over it" campaign in schools to challenge homophobic language among pupils.

sources.label: High Speed Training — History of LGBTQ+ Timeline

title: First Trans Pride event is held in Brighton
description: Brighton hosted the first Trans Pride event in 2013, drawing around 450 participants and described as the first of its kind in Europe.

sources.label: High Speed Training — History of LGBTQ+ Timeline

title: Same-sex marriage takes effect in England and Wales
description: The Marriage (Same-Sex Couples) Act came into effect in 2014, allowing same-sex couples to marry in England and Wales. Scotland introduced same-sex marriage later the same year.

sources.label: High Speed Training — History of LGBTQ+ Timeline

title: Northern Ireland lifts the one-year MSM blood donation ban
description: In 2016 Northern Ireland removed the one-year deferral on blood donation by men who have sex with men, bringing it into line with the rest of the United Kingdom.

sources.label: High Speed Training — History of LGBTQ+ Timeline

title: Relationships and sex education made mandatory in schools
description: In 2017 an amendment to the Children and Social Work Bill made relationships and sex education compulsory in all schools in England and Wales, effective from 2019.

sources.label: High Speed Training — History of LGBTQ+ Timeline

title: MSM blood donation deferral cut from one year to three months
description: In 2017 most of the United Kingdom shortened the blood donation deferral for men who have sex with men from one year to three months, while Northern Ireland kept the one-year rule.

sources.label: High Speed Training — History of LGBTQ+ Timeline

title: World Health Organisation declassifies being transgender as a mental illness
description: In 2019 the World Health Organisation removed transgender identity from its list of mental disorders in the ICD-11, reclassifying it as a matter of sexual health.

sources.label: High Speed Training — History of LGBTQ+ Timeline

title: First London Trans Pride march is held
description: London held its first Trans Pride march in 2019, attracting more than 1,500 participants.

sources.label: High Speed Training — History of LGBTQ+ Timeline

title: Same-sex marriage legalised in Northern Ireland
description: Same-sex marriage was legalised in Northern Ireland in 2019, with the first ceremony taking place on 11 February 2020. The change made marriage equality available across the whole United Kingdom.

sources.label: High Speed Training — History of LGBTQ+ Timeline
